prose:
**Fukui reactivity analysis of benzaldehyde** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, PySCF; density fitting on by default), **gas phase**, neutral reference charge 0 / multiplicity 1. The N±1 states used for the finite-difference Fukui functions were the cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anion (charge −1, multiplicity 2), computed as open-shell (UKS). Geometry: user-supplied `.xyz` (`benzaldehyde.xyz`, 14 atoms), used as-is (single-point Fukui analysis — the geometry was NOT re-optimized at this level). Partial charges used to build the Fukui indices were Mulliken (PySCF).

**Method used:** `fukui-reactivity` skill with `--method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard` (gas phase).

**Headline result:** The most electrophilic site (largest f⁺, susceptible to nucleophilic attack) is **atom index 3 (1-based), a carbon**, with **f⁺ = 0.39939**. The most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻, susceptible to electrophilic attack) is **atom index 1, the carbonyl oxygen**, with **f⁻ = 0.23840**.

Note on atom numbering: the atom listing is `[O, C, C, C, C, C, C, C, H×6]`, so atom index 3 is a ring/carbonyl carbon and atom index 1 is the O.

**Integrity / convergence:** The engine reports `integrity.status = ok` and **trustworthy = true**. Both Fukui charge-conservation checks passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f⁻) = 1.0000, indicating the N±1 states converged properly.

**Warnings (reproduced verbatim from the result JSON `integrity.checks`):**
- fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge) — check ok
- fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge) — check ok
- schema_header: common header present — check ok

(There was no separate `warnings` array in the result JSON; the above are the integrity-check details, all of which passed.)

**Reproducibility:** Command run — `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz`. A Fukui plot was written to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_claude-opus-4.8/20260702-200708_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.

Caveats per reporting standards: the Fukui functions are finite-difference (ΔN = ±1) descriptors built from Mulliken charges, which are basis-set dependent and only qualitatively reliable; the analysis is on the supplied (non-re-optimized) geometry in implicit-free gas phase; and B3LYP/def2-TZVP uses density fitting. No experimental comparison is offered.
